skill.name: pdf-to-ppt-pack
skill.description: Convert research paper PDFs into literature-report PPTX decks using a fully offline workflow (extract text/figures, map captions, summarize findings, and generate slides). Use when you need to turn a PDF into a presentation deck, especially for scientific articles with figures and tables.
skill.summary: Turn research paper PDFs into structured PowerPoint presentations with extracted figures, captions, and summaries using a fully offline workflow.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The skill is a markdown documentation file that describes a PDF-to-PPT conversion workflow. All 55 static analysis findings are false positives: the shell commands appear inside markdown code blocks as documentation examples, the URL is a source attribution link, and the weak crypto algorithm alerts result from the scanner misidentifying normal documentation text. No executable code, network requests, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was detected.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged shell command examples inside markdown code blocks as Ruby backtick execution. All flagged lines (e.g., python commands in bash code fences, powershell code fence markers) are documentation examples, not executed code. No command injection or unauthorized execution risk exists.

low_findings.title: Source attribution URL misidentified as network risk
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a GitHub repository URL in the source attribution line as a network risk. This is a standard markdown link to the skill's source repository and does not initiate network requests during execution. No exfiltration or unauthorized network call risk.

low_findings.title: Normal documentation text misidentified as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 10 lines of markdown documentation text as containing weak cryptographic algorithm references. The flagged lines contain only plain English descriptions (e.g., 'Builds a PPTX deck following predefined layout rules', 'One slide per figure with concise interpretation'). No cryptographic algorithms, encoding, or hashing functions are present in the file.

low_findings.title: Documentation text misidentified as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 47 which reads 'Output discipline: keep results reproducible, identify assumptions explicitly, and avoid undocumented side effects.' This is standard documentation about output quality practices, not system reconnaissance or probing instructions.

content.user_title: Convert Research Paper PDFs to PowerPoint Presentations
content.value_statement: Turning research paper PDFs into presentation slides is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates the process by extracting text and figures from PDFs, mapping captions to images, and generating structured PPTX decks ready for journal clubs and literature reviews.
actual_capabilities: Extract figure images and text content from research paper PDFs
Map figure captions and legends to extracted images automatically
Generate structured PPTX slides with title, overview, per-figure, and summary sections
Support iterative refinement by editing figure titles and regenerating the deck
Process PDFs fully offline with no external API or service dependencies
limitations: Requires Python 3.10+ and the python-pptx and lxml libraries installed locally
Cannot process scanned PDFs that lack embedded extractable text
Slide layout and styling is limited to the predefined template structure
Figure extraction quality depends on the source PDF's embedded image resolution

use_cases.title: Journal Club Presentation
use_cases.description: A student or researcher needs to present a recent paper at journal club. The skill extracts figures and text from the PDF and builds slides with one figure per slide plus interpretation, saving hours of manual slide creation.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate students and postdocs preparing journal club talks

use_cases.title: Literature Review Compilation
use_cases.description: A researcher compiling multiple papers for a literature review section can convert each PDF into consistent slide decks. The structured output makes it easy to compare findings across papers.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers writing literature reviews for grants or publications

use_cases.title: Lab Meeting Reports
use_cases.description: A scientist preparing a weekly lab meeting update can convert a newly published paper into slides with figures and summaries, enabling quick sharing of relevant findings with the team.
use_cases.target_user: Principal investigators and lab members sharing recent papers

prompt_templates.title: Convert a single PDF to a presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a research paper PDF at [path/to/paper.pdf]. Please extract the figures and text, and generate a PPTX presentation with a title slide, overview slide, one slide per figure with interpretation, and a summary slide. Save the output to [path/to/output.pptx].
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a specific PDF and wants a complete slide deck generated in one step

prompt_templates.title: Refine figure titles and regenerate
prompt_templates.prompt: The titles in my generated presentation are too long. I have edited the figure_titles_zh.txt file in [path/to/offline_dir]. Please rerun the deck generation step using these updated titles and produce a new PPTX at [path/to/output.pptx].
prompt_templates.scenario: User reviewed the initial output and wants to improve slide titles before the final version

prompt_templates.title: Extract figures only without building slides
prompt_templates.prompt: I only need to extract the figures and text from [path/to/paper.pdf] into an offline directory. Please run the extraction script and save the output to [path/to/offline_dir]. I will review the extracted content before building slides.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to inspect extracted figures and captions before committing to a slide layout

prompt_templates.title: Batch process multiple papers for a review
prompt_templates.prompt: I have several research paper PDFs in [path/to/papers/]. Please process each one into its own offline directory and then generate a combined PPTX presentation that has a common title slide, an overview of all papers, individual slides for each paper's figures, and a comparative summary at the end.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user preparing a multi-paper review presentation such as for a grant or thesis committee

output_examples.input: Convert a 6-page Nature paper PDF about CRISPR gene editing into a presentation
output_examples.output: A 9-slide PPTX deck containing: Slide 1 - Title slide with paper title, journal, and date. Slide 2 - Overview slide with background and key findings plus graphical abstract. Slides 3-7 - One slide per extracted figure (Figure 1-5) with the image on the left and a concise interpretation paragraph on the right. Slide 8 - Summary of main conclusions and limitations. Slide 9 - References slide.

output_examples.input: I need to fix the figure titles on my generated deck. The titles are too long and wordy.
output_examples.output: After editing the figure_titles_zh.txt file to contain short labels like 'Figure 1: Study Design' and 'Figure 2: Survival Analysis', the regeneration step produces a clean PPTX where each slide header is brief and readable, with the full description moved to the speaker notes section.

output_examples.input: Extract figures from a clinical trial paper PDF for review before building slides
output_examples.output: The extraction phase creates a directory containing: Figure_1.jpg through Figure_6.jpg, Graphical_abstract.jpg (if detected), extracted_text.txt with the full paper text, and figure_legend_hits.txt listing detected caption-to-image mappings. The user can review these files before proceeding to slide generation.

best_practices: Verify the PDF has selectable (not scanned) text before starting the extraction phase to avoid silent failures
Review figure titles and caption mappings after extraction and edit figure_titles_zh.txt before final slide generation
Confirm all required Python packages (python-pptx, lxml) are installed before running any scripts
anti_patterns: Do not skip the extraction review step before building slides - captions may need manual correction
Do not attempt to process book-length PDFs or documents with hundreds of pages as a single request
Do not modify the offline directory structure after extraction as the deck generator expects a specific layout

faq.question: What Python version do I need to run the scripts?
faq.answer: Python 3.10 or higher is required. The scripts depend on python-pptx version 0.6.21+ and lxml version 4.9.0+.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: No. The workflow requires PDFs with embedded selectable text. Scanned documents (images only) cannot be processed because the extraction tools need machine-readable text.

faq.question: Does this skill send my PDF data to any external service?
faq.answer: No. The pipeline runs entirely offline. All processing happens locally using Python scripts installed in the repository.

faq.question: How do I customize the slide layout or colors?
faq.answer: Customization is limited. The deck generation follows a predefined template with title, overview, per-figure, and summary slides. For layout changes, you would need to modify the PPT building scripts in the repository.

faq.question: Can I process multiple papers into a single presentation?
faq.answer: Yes, by running the extraction phase on each paper separately and then combining outputs manually. The skill is designed for single-paper decks but can be adapted for multi-paper reviews.

faq.question: What should I do if a figure extraction fails or produces a wrong image?
faq.answer: Review the figure_legend_hits.txt file to check caption-to-image mappings. Some PDFs embed figures in non-standard ways. You can edit the mapping file and rerun the extraction phase as needed.
